title: Expose workflow skills in Codex app
summary: Make .agents/skills the only active workflow surface so Codex discovers and invokes workflow operations as skills, and audit the workflow-building system so operator-facing docs and package surfaces stop presenting npx, Python, npm, or raw helper commands as the primary way Ace uses skills in Codex, Claude Code, opencode, or Antigravity-style agent runtimes.
acceptance_criteria: Full canonical skill instructions live under .agents/skills
continue-task exists as a discoverable skill alias
active prompt and command shim directories are removed
workflow help documents the skills-only surface
operator-facing skill docs lead with $skill-name invocation and Codex-native behavior, not npx, Python, npm, or raw helper commands
helper commands are documented only as internal implementation details, explicit verification steps, or developer package smoke tests when a task deliberately owns a package surface
project/package docs distinguish Codex-native operator use from developer/package installation or smoke-test use
workflow packages are framed as skill packages for agent runtimes such as Codex, Claude Code, opencode, and Antigravity-style environments, not as CLI-first tools
.agents/skills remains the active local Codex skill surface for normal Ace/Codex use
project-local packaged skills remain exportable skill-package artifacts for other agent runtimes, but their docs still lead with skill/runtime usage rather than command invocation
the task audits active skills, project-local packaged skills, project READMEs, workflow-help text, validation rules, and recent workflow-foundry artifacts for command-first skill-calling language
the implementation plan defines what must be rewritten, what can remain as developer verification, and what validation should prevent from recurring
the audit classifies existing package.json, bin, CLI, npm, npx, Python, and raw helper surfaces before any deletion or quarantine decision
deletion is not the default first move; prevention and explicit skill-first rules are the default, with delete/quarantine reserved for surfaces that clearly violate the workflow contract
root AGENTS.md becomes the primary source of truth for the skill-first/agent-runtime-first rule

active workflow skills repeat the skill-first rule where it affects behavior, especially workflow-help, setup-workflow-project, initiate-task, continue-task, audit-foundry, testing-session, review-project-state, and workflow-tracker-ui
the repeated guidance stays concise and points back to root AGENTS.md instead of creating conflicting local policies
implementation sequences the root AGENTS.md rule and validator before repairing individual skills, READMEs, package docs, or project-local skill surfaces
all audited surfaces that violate the approved rule are repaired in this task when phase guard approvals cover the paths
validation hard-fails where feasible when docs or skill/package surfaces present npx, Python, npm, or raw helper commands as the primary way to call a workflow skill
workflow artifacts default to skills, Markdown, and JSON; .mjs exists only for deterministic validators, query helpers, or internal runtime support called by skills
new workflow packages must target agent runtimes such as Codex, Claude Code, opencode, and Antigravity-style environments before any CLI/package runner surface
validation fails prompt, command, legacy skill, malformed skill, and folder/name mismatch fixtures
workflow state validation passes

session_log.event: Created workflow-change task to track Codex app slash prompt exposure for ace-workflows.
Added .agents/skills entrypoints because Codex skill discovery uses the skill surface, while .codex/prompts are compatibility prompts.
Updated snapshot to include workflow-foundry-004 after adding tracker-maintenance task for tracker upkeep.
Converted the task from prompt exposure to skills-only canonical workflow surfaces and removed command/prompt shim artifacts.
Updated snapshot to include workflow-foundry-005 after adding the dark-mode tracker UI intake task.
Updated snapshot to include workflow-foundry-006 after adding the foundry expansion discovery intake task.

Continued task into grilling after user identified a foundry-level setup problem: workflow surfaces keep implying Ace should run npx, Python, npm, or raw helper commands, while the intended operator model is Codex-native use through AGENTS.md and .agents/skills with $skill-name invocation.

Grilling decision: calling skills in this repo means invoking Codex-discoverable skills through the skill surface. npx, npm, Python, or raw helper commands may exist for installation, package checks, deterministic validation, or developer smoke tests, but they must not be presented as the normal operator-facing way to call a skill.

Grilling decision: the scope is complete workflow-building posture, not a narrow README wording fix. This repo builds and packages skills/workflows for agent runtimes such as Codex, Claude Code, opencode, and Antigravity-style environments; the task must audit active skills, project-local packaged skills, READMEs, workflow-help text, validation, and recent foundry artifacts for command-first skill-calling assumptions.

Grilling decision: make command-first skill usage a hard validator failure as much as possible. The foundry builds skills, Markdown, and JSON workflow state; .mjs may exist for deterministic validators, query helpers, or internal support that skills call, but workflow products should target agent runtimes such as Codex, Claude Code, opencode, and Antigravity-style environments before any CLI or package-runner surface.

Grilling decision: do not default to deleting package.json bin or CLI-like surfaces blindly. First audit and classify package.json, bin, CLI, npm, npx, Python, and raw helper surfaces. The main fix is prevention and explicit skill-first rules: the repo builds skills/workflows for agent runtimes such as opencode, Claude Code, Codex, and Antigravity-style environments, so command surfaces must be justified as validation, packaging metadata, or internal support rather than treated as the product.

Grilling decision: put the skill-first/agent-runtime-first rule in top-level AGENTS.md as the source of truth, and repeat concise reminders in active workflow skills where the rule affects behavior. The reminders should say the foundry primarily builds skills/workflows to install into and run through agent runtimes such as opencode, Claude Code, Codex, and Antigravity-style environments, not command-first npx/Python workflows.

Grilling decision: sequence implementation as root AGENTS.md source-of-truth rule plus validator first, then repair each skill, README, package doc, or project-local skill surface found by the audit list in the same task when approvals cover the paths.

Grilling decision: use a two-layer skill model. .agents/skills is the active local Codex surface for normal Ace/Codex use. Project-local packaged skills can remain exportable skill-package artifacts for other agent runtimes, but their docs must still be skill-first and agent-runtime-first rather than command-first.
Moved to PRD after user approved the recommended PRD artifact. Wrote the skill-first runtime surface PRD and set issues as the next action.

Split the PRD into four local issue artifacts covering the root rule, validator guard, surface repairs, and validation/review/push closeout. Moved task to issues with implement as the next action after user asked to continue until pushed.

Moved to implement with approved artifacts for the root AGENTS.md rule, active workflow skill reminders, validator hard-fail behavior, Real Life Workflows package docs repair, and package-surface validator alignment.

Implemented the skill-first runtime surface rule, added section-aware command-first validation, repaired active skill and Real Life Workflows package docs, linked audit report validation blockers to workflow-foundry-011, and wrote the focused code-review artifact.
Completed workflow-foundry-003 after validation, focused review, and implementation checkpoint commit; final tracker closeout is ready for push.
